clean_text: i absolutely loved this hotel!!! one of my favorites of all time. the location is superb - we wanted posh, and we got posh! street-noise was low despite being a fairly busy street and it was extremely easy to get around from the hotel. the staff were all lovely and the rooms were beautiful and luxurious. we were warmly welcomed back to the hotel every time with the door held open and the staff was warm, chatting with us about things they recommend checking out. the bed/pillows was one of my favorite aspects of the hotel - so comfy and a delight to fall into after a long day of exploring. i also appreciated the little things they provided such as comfy robes, slippers, sturdy umbrellas, and some complementary mini-bar treats. we initially had an issue with the window not closing fully but the staff came instantly and fixed it right up. they were overall so quick with any request we had (such as extra coffee pods, extra silverware) and the room was lovingly cleaned and linens replaced every day. some things to note but not negatives: the infrastructure is a bit older than some modern hotels, but i found it very charming overall (i.e. the heat/ac controls were a little tricky, the lights were on a funny timer, the room was small-ish, and we could hear some upstairs noise). nothing deal-breaking at all, just some quirks :) i found this hotel to be a very fair price for what it offers, it lives up to the expectations of a 5-star hotel, and i would absolutely stay here again given the chance. missing it already!

clean_text: please note: this review is predominantly about the customer service at head office and the general running of fitness first. while i can’t say anything particularly positive about the staff at the gyms, i also can’t say anything overtly negative. over the two years i was a member of fitness first, i changed gyms three times. i started at the tottenham court road gym and was genuinely impressed by the facilities and what it offered. however, this location closed, and although we were expected to pay the same membership fee at the new gym, there was a significant drop in standards. this was the marylebone location, which i eventually left due to how poor it was, not enough facilities, unhelpful staff, and absolutely not worth the price being charged. the selling points of the cold plunge and sauna were irrelevant as the sauna was smaller than tottenham court road and the cold plunge didnt work over the 3 months i was there. i then moved to the oxford circus gym. while it is very small, there is a good community of regulars. that said, it gets extremely busy, and it’s often not an ideal environment to train in. the biggest issue with fitness first, however, is how the company is run. there is no genuine customer interaction, and there seems to be a large disconnect from what i think should be more than a bog-standard gym. head office refuses to speak on the phone and can take up to a week to respond to emails. when challenged about being forced to move gyms, you’re simply told you’re tied into a contract and therefore can’t leave. i recently decided that i was tired of paying a high price for the bare minimum, so i chose to leave. the repercussions of this decision have been a nightmare. i cancelled through the app and was told i had nothing left to pay. a few days later, i received an email stating my account was overdue. when i called the oxford circus gym (the only location you can actually phone), i was met with a sarcastic response: “you should know you can’t just cancel your direct debit.” this is not what i did, nor what i would ever do. this situation ultimately resulted in complete silence from head office once they became involved, and i was left having to pay regardless of the circumstances. i would not recommend fitness first if you’re looking to elevate your fitness experience beyond that of a cheap commercial gym. fitness first is exactly that — a cheap commercial gym, just with a much higher monthly cost. i do not recommend.

clean_text: i joined this gym based on the volume of positive reviews, but in reality it has been a major disappointment and represents extremely poor value for money, particularly at ~£109 per month. the facilities are in a poor state. the gym feels persistently damp, and much of the equipment shows visible rust and wear. the range of equipment is also limited compared to other gyms at this price point, making it difficult to train properly without compromise. the most serious issue is the changing facilities. the male changing rooms are currently closed for renovation. they were sub-standard before, and the temporary solution is frankly unacceptable. members are told to change in a tiny room in the corner of the gym, which realistically fits no more than three people. on my last visit, we were crammed into this space, no room to move, no privacy, and very limited secure storage. there are only a handful of lockers available. it completely undermines the service being paid for. despite this, members are still being charged full price. the two-month cancellation notice period is not clearly highlighted during sign-up. it may be in the small print, but it is certainly not obvious, which only adds to the sense of poor transparency. overall, this is one of the worst gym facilities i have used relative to cost. unless there is a significant improvement or acknowledgement of the reduced service currently being offered, i cannot recommend this gym to anyone.

clean_text: having struggled with acne for years i finally made an appointment to see a dermatologist and was excited to start taking steps toward healing my skin. unfortunately i left feeling deflated and extremely dissappointed. to spend such a huge amount of money and wait for a month from the time of booking to be in the room for less than 4 minutes with a specialist who offered no advice, asked almost no questions and barely looked at me apart from to shove an ipad in my face to take pictures feels like some sort of prank. the lack of empathy and depth of inquiry for somebody coming to you for advice for a condition that can affect your confidence and quality of life was truly upsetting and i cried outside. accutane was suggested as a treatment but not discussed in detail (hard to discuss anything in detail in 4 minutes) and i left with a prescription for a topical gel that my doctor has given me on the nhs (for free) 3 times already that stops working when the course of treatment is over. i feel as though i was given two choices: accutane or the gel and to go on accutane requires intensive health checks first and isn't an avenue i want to explore. i thought i would receive recommendations for products to use as well as lifestyle advice and maybe learn something about my skin type or a new way i can try to heal my skin however i feel as though i was just another appointment to get out of the way and another chunk of money to make from someone's suffering. if people come to you as a specialist with a medical problem they are struggling with, i truly feel they should be made to feel listened to and not rushed or dismissed, that's why people go private. to pay an additional £40 on top of the appointment fee for something i have been prescribed by a doctor 3 times already for free feels like i have actually been robbed. my gp has offered more sympathy and solutions despite clearly not being as well funded as this clinic and in the future i will not be coming back here for treatment or advice as i left with neither and was £250 out of pocket. having now read some reviews i can see that this is an ongoing problem specifically for acne appointments and that other people have left feeling the same as me. as a wellness clinic i truly hope people start getting the care they deserve from you.

clean_text: let me be clear! i was surprised by the number of negative reviews, but as someone who visits gay bathhouses wherever i travel, i had to stop by during my visit to manchester. unfortunately, i couldn’t enter the venue this time because i forgot my id (and yes, it’s standard practice for bathhouses across most european countries to ask for identification, which is perfectly fine). i’ll come back and update my review once i actually experience the venue itself. but one important thing: there are dozens of negative reviews about the receptionist, the “guy with glasses,” as many call him. well, i met him too, and we had a lovely chat. i asked him about the negative feedback, and he explained who usually writes those reviews (guess who is not allowed to enter and gets pissed 🚭🚯💊💉). he also clarified the so‑called “cold shower” issue, which makes total sense: when 100+ men on busy days keep pressing the shower buttons constantly, hot water for the boiler doesn’t always have enough time to reheat the water. so it’s not the staff’s fault; it’s just how the system works in their case. take a quick shower - you’re not at home! overall, i had a great impression of the guy. so please, keep your judgment balanced when leaving negative reviews. i’ll definitely be back. p.s. towels and condoms are free. flip-flops (not disposable ones, but proper reusable plastic ones you can keep) cost £3. keep up the gay culture manchester. much love!

clean_text: my husband and i enjoyed a short break at this boutique hotel in central manchester in june 2025. ideally located - a quiet street with v little noise but only a 5 minute walk away from bustling bars, shops and restaurants. we enjoyed breakfast and room service within the hotel itself with a great choice offered for both. i visited their spa which is based in the adjoining building. i enjoyed a blissful massage and as a guest of the hotel was then able to stay and enjoy the other communal facilities of the spa on a complimentary basis. what probably sets this hotel apart from other boutique hotels in the area is their lovely rooftop infinity pool. with great views and open air (during the warm weather) it felt really special. being a converted georgian building there is only one lift (elevator) which is small. this is often in use by the hotel’s staff (housekeeping etc) so you may have to wait a little while. no parking on site but discounted parking a short walk away. the staff were really warm and friendly. would definitely stay here again.

clean_text: cash flow alert!! sadly, this lovely hotel does pre-authorisation on your bank card on check in - for the amount of the rooms plus £100 per person deposit. you pay on departure as normal. but they don't cancel the pre-authorisation! of course this affects your available balance. we checked out on tuesday morning, but pre-authorisation for nearly £800 wasn't removed until friday, in spite of us contacting the hotel several times. so you need to budget for twice the cost of the hotel for 3 or 4 days. sad, because the rooms are lovely, staff on reception and in the bar are friendly and the location is great - notably the wonderful 10 tib lane bar/restaurant round the corner. but this practice spoiled it for us and we'd never recommend it to friends for that reason.

clean_text: i had a pleasant visit to amala spa, however i was surprised to hear that treatments are not available during the week, which was slightly disappointing. given the treatments were not running, it felt more like a health club than a spa as there are no relaxation areas, hot drinks, darker lighting, or any other amenities typically associated with the spa experience. saying that, the facilities included a small gym, a nice 1.68m deep pool ideal for some short lengths, a boiling hot sauna, a small steam room, a small jacuzzi, and a plunge pool (though it wasn’t particularly cold - the dedicated cold shower was much colder!). there are several beds/chairs to relax in, and the overall atmosphere is nice and calm. the changing rooms are a decent size and offer combination lockers, but many of them were broken - it took me four attempts to find one that worked. i arrived later in the evening, and the changing area was showing signs of use throughout the day, with towels, sandals, and a left over swimming costume with bottles etc. left in the showers. one of the shower heads was also on the floor. one of the showers also didn’t get hot, so i had to switch showers to one that worked. overall, it’s a nice space to unwind if you’re staying in birmingham for an event at the icc or rec, but it could definitely benefit from more regular upkeep throughout the day, as well as a few maintenance issues being fixed such as the lockers and the showers. it looks to be showing its age a little bit.

clean_text: we stayed at alamo guest house and had mixed feelings. the location and the historic house have potential, but for the price (about £250 per night), we expected more. on the downside: you need to ring to get in, and there are only stairs to both entrance and rooms. breakfast has to be reserved for a fixed time, which feels a bit restrictive. we unfortunately had a 24-hour flight delay and, although the booking was non-refundable, we provided proof of the cancellation. a goodwill gesture (like refunding the missed breakfasts) would have been appreciated, but nothing was offered. the rooms are carpeted throughout – which is a matter of taste – and we ended up in a basement room. while no “park view” was advertised, a basement wasn’t what we expected for this price. no bathrobe was provided, which would have been useful given the shared bathroom. the toilet flushed poorly (needing long, hard pressing of the button). coffee at breakfast was very disappointing (just french press and not tasty). overall, it felt expensive for what you get. on the positive side: the young woman at check-in and check-out was very friendly, as was the breakfast staff. the beds weren’t very large, but we slept well. even from the basement, we had a partial view of trees. the bathroom was pleasantly warm, and the à la carte breakfast itself was tasty.

clean_text: my partner and i have been members here since august 2025, and honestly, i can't imagine training anywhere else in bristol now. we initially prioritized nuffield for the wet facilities–the 20m pool, sauna, and steam room are excellent–but the variety of the gym floor and group classes has kept us hooked. from body pump to pilates, the quality of instruction in the two studios is consistently top-tier. the staff are genuinely friendly and the complimentary towel service is a brilliant touch that makes the experience feel much smoother. however, to give a complete picture: the facility is showing its age slightly. the lockers are on the smaller side, and you won't find a hyper-modern or "fancy" aesthetic here. it feels like a standard, established uk club rather than a futuristic luxury spa–which is fairly typical and perfectly acceptable, but worth noting if you are expecting brand-new fittings. the pool does get busy during peak hours (after work and weekends), but with a little patience, it is always manageable. the membership is at a premium price point, but if you are consistent with your routine, it is hands down the best investment you can make for yourself.

clean_text: i’ve been a member of nuffield clifton on and off for over 15 years. unfortunately, it’s clear that the facility has not seen meaningful maintenance or upgrades during that time, despite consistent and significant membership price increases. it now appears the cost of membership is around £115 a month – pricing that suggests a premium experience, but the reality is far from that. facilities and cleanliness: the gym feels outdated and poorly maintained. the swimming pool water often looks murky and gives off an odd smell. while it seems a “cleaning team” has been introduced in response to negative reviews, their efforts don’t appear thorough. surfaces might get wiped down, but deeper cleaning is clearly being neglected. the changing rooms are especially disappointing. dirty, wet towels are often left lying around. the cover sheets in changing rooms are visibly caked in muck, makeup, and grime. showers frequently contain hair, plasters, and general residue. lockers are outdated and could benefit from a full refurbishment. the cleanliness of the shower area continues to be a concern. there is frequently visible hair in the drains and on the floors, suggesting that these areas are not being adequately cleaned or checked throughout the day. the showers themselves appear old and rusted, with grime accumulating around the fittings, making them look neglected and unhygienic. in the changing rooms, the sheet pullovers used on benches are often stained with make-up, dirt, and general grime, which is not only off-putting but raises concerns about sanitation standards. small but noticeable issues such as paint peeling near the hand dryers and a faulty shower cubicle door — which is almost coming off. classes and studios: pilates and other studio spaces are also poorly managed. one of the pilates rooms currently contains building equipment scattered across the floor. in a recent class, there was even a pot noodle left in the corner – not quite the calm, clean environment you’d expect from a health and wellbeing venue. gym equipment and amenities: many machines do not support bluetooth connectivity, and screens that used to play sky sports have been removed. this removes a once-popular feature that added to the gym experience. suggested improvements: • clean the changing rooms regularly and thoroughly – not surface wipes, but a full, scheduled clean with accountability. • swimming pool loungers – invest in heated ones like other clubs offer. • sports access on gym screens – reinstate sky sports or a similar subscription for gymgoers. • steam room – update it fully, not just patch repairs. • make the cleaning team effective – provide training or oversight to ensure the job is done properly. • locker replacement – update the lockers to provide a more modern, functional experience. • general maintenance – ensure studio spaces are free from construction gear or leftover food. • transparency on pricing – if fees increase, members deserve to see tangible improvements.
